page.content: The Asia-Pacific region has witnessed a notable shift in consumer preferences towards plant-based products, particularly within the foodservice channel.

lines.text: The Asia-Pacific region has witnessed a notable shift in consumer preferences towards plant-based products, particularly within

page.content: As indicated by the Market Share (%) metrics for 2023 and 2024, the adoption of plant-based options among quick-service restaurants (QSR) is becoming increasingly significant, with a market share projected to rise from 10.1% to 12.5%. This trend reflects a growing demand for sustainable and health-conscious food choices, which is particularly evident in various segments of the foodservice industry.

lines.text: As indicated by the Market Share (%) metrics for 2023 and 2024, the adoption of plant-based options among quick-service
restaurants (QSR) is becoming increasingly significant, with a market share projected to rise from 10.1% to 12.5%. This trend
reflects a growing demand for sustainable and health-conscious food choices, which is particularly evident in various segments

page.content: For instance, the QSR segment is expected to enhance its market presence as cafes report a robust increase in plant-based offerings, rising from 10.0% in the past period. Meanwhile, the catering segment is also adapting, albeit at a slower pace, with a current market share of 2.8%, which underscores the varying degrees of acceptance across different foodservice channels. Additionally, the overall growth in the plant-based food sector is anticipated to be supported by strategic initiatives aimed at expanding product availability; for example, the number of outlets offering these items is projected to increase from 600 to 1200. As stakeholders navigate these trends, understanding the dynamics behind such changes will be crucial for capitalizing on the emerging opportunities within this vibrant market landscape.
